title: Ransomware attack on IRORG
description:
The Security Operations Center (SOC) begins receiving a sequence of high-severity alerts
from multiple endpoints within the IROrg network. Shortly after the alerts begin, employees report that shared folders on srv-web01 are inaccessible or populated with files bearing an unfamiliar extension: .df_locked. A ransom note appears in affected directories demanding cryptocurrency payment for data recovery.
tasks:
[{"createdBy": "responder7@irorg.com", "createdAt": "23/04/2026 11:30", "title": "Detection", "description": "The team detected a series of alerts through the SIEM starting on 24th February 2026 09:12:33 UTC. The SOC noticed activity from many endpoints within a short timeframe and execution of unknown binaries from TEMP directories. The SOC also noticed encoded powershell commands and outbound connections to external IPs.

Shortly after, employees noticed that shared folders on **srv-web01** were inaccessible and had a lot of files with .df_locked extension. A ransom note appears in affected directories demanding cryptocurrency payment for data recovery.", "owner": "responder7@irorg.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "23/04/2026 11:32", "endDate": "23/04/2026 11:34"}, {"createdBy": "responder7@irorg.com", "createdAt": "23/04/2026 11:31", "title": "Recovery", "description": "Following containment, We reimaged ws-user03 and ws-user04 from clean baselines and rebuilt srv-web01. We disabled the admin_ir account and rotated all privileged credentials on dc01. For the encrypted files, We checked for Volume Shadow Copies and submitted df.exe to ID Ransomware to identify a potential decryptor. As srv-backup01 was compromised and unreliable, files with no recovery path were documented as unrecoverable and escalated to management.

=== TASK ACTIVITY HISTORY ===

--- LOG ENTRY ---
ID: ~41042096
Author: responder7@irorg.com
Date: 23/04/2026 13:04
Type: case_task_log
Message: -  We reimaged ws-user03 and ws-user04 from clean baselines.
- We rebuilt srv-web01 from a clean baseline and manually identified and prioritised critical encrypted files for recovery.
- We brought systems back online incrementally, validating each host against a clean baseline before reconnecting to the network.
- We checked for Volume Shadow Copies (VSS) on srv-web01 and used  pre-encryption snapshots.
- We disabled the admin_ir account and rotated all privileged credentials on dc01.
- We submitted df.exe to ID Ransomware to identify a potential decryptor.
- For files with no recovery path, I documented them as unrecoverable and reported the data loss to management for business continuity planning.", "owner": "responder7@irorg.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "23/04/2026 13:04", "endDate": "23/04/2026 13:07"}, {"createdBy": "responder7@irorg.com", "createdAt": "23/04/2026 11:30", "title": "Analysis", "description": "From the Analysis, The files involved in the alerts are all related to Ryuk Ransomware: Extensive Attack Infrastructure. Ryuk Ransomware: Extensive Attack Infrastructure is the threat group.
They created the process with update_213.exe, used svchost32.exe as the command and control module, Cleared event logs and obtained admin control to the domain controller (admin_ir).
df.exe is the encryption module and it was used to encrypt files in the share folder.

Located and deleted all identified payloads
* update_213.exe 
* svchost32.exe
* credscan.exe
* df.exe

along with any associated registry entries. Terminated all running instances of the encryption module on **srv-web01.irorg.local** to stop further file locking, and remove any persistence mechanisms such as scheduled tasks, startup entries, or hidden backdoors used to maintain access.

Revoked compromised access by resetting the **admin_ir** account and any accounts involved in unauthorized activity on **dc01.irorg.local**. Rotate all service credentials, API tokens, and cryptographic keys potentially exposed by **credscan.exe**. Audit Active Directory, remove unauthorized accounts, and strip any illicit privilege escalations.

Rebuild heavily compromised systems like **ws-user03** and **ws-user04** from clean, trusted images to ensure complete removal of threats. Patch all systems to eliminate exploited entry points, especially around TEMP directory abuse and PowerShell execution. Enforce permanent network-level blocks against known C2 endpoints such as **142.93.122.23**.

Validate eradication by running full-scale vulnerability scans across internal and external assets using **Cortex**, then perform repeat verification checks to confirm all malicious artifacts, access paths, and ransomware components are fully eliminated.


=== TASK ACTIVITY HISTORY ===

- Metrics collection", "owner": "responder6@irorg.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "23/04/2026 13:11", "endDate": "23/04/2026 13:16"}, {"createdBy": "responder7@irorg.com", "createdAt": "23/04/2026 11:31", "title": "Containment", "description": "I will immediately start isolating **ws-user03**, **ws-user04**, **dc01**, and **srv-web01** from the network to stop .df_locked encryption and cut off C2 traffic to **88.119.174.129** and **142.93.122.23**. Terminate all active sessions and disable the** admin_ir** account due to suspicious workstation-based logins. Block all outbound communication to known malicious infrastructure, including **servicewikii.com**, **onixcellent.com**, and **chishir.com**, at the firewall and DNS level.

I will also enforce network segmentation by isolating critical systems like **srv-backup01** into a restricted VLAN to prevent lateral movement. Initiate a full credential reset across all administrative and service accounts to invalidate any compromised access, especially those exposed via tools like **credscan.exe**. Capture forensic images and memory snapshots of impacted systems before remediation to preserve evidence of binaries such as update_213.exe and svchost32.exe.

Audit **srv-backup01** for any unauthorized connections, particularly to **95.179.219.169**, to rule out data exfiltration. Maintain isolation of srv-web01 while shifting essential operations to controlled manual processes until the system is securely rebuilt.

Conduct root cause analysis by tracing the initial compromise on **ws-user03**, validating whether the entry vector was phishing, drive-by download, or exploitation of an unpatched vulnerability. Identify control failures by analyzing why endpoint defenses did not block staging binaries and how **credscan.exe** successfully harvested **admin_ir** credentials. Review SOC visibility gaps, particularly around missed indicators such as cleared Windows event logs on compromised systems.

Drive corrective actions by updating the incident response plan and ransomware playbooks to reflect observed attacker behavior and improve response speed. Strengthen security controls by enforcing stricter patch management across endpoints and servers, deploying phishing-resistant MFA to protect administrative access, and tightening PowerShell execution policies to block abuse of encoded commands.
